title: Complete Medieval History – GV Sir Headshot Series
source: GvKaksha / Eduquity Pattern – SSC CGL/CHSL/MTS/Delhi Police 2026

table.sub_areas: Invasions, battles, writers at court, Prithviraj Chauhan
Qutub-ud-din Aibak, Iltutmish, Razia Sultan, Balban, Chahalgani
Alauddin Khilji, Market Reforms, Malik Kafur, battles
Ghiyas-ud-din, Muhammad Bin Tughlaq, Firoz Shah Tughlaq
Bahlol Lodhi, Sikander Lodhi, Ibrahim Lodhi
Kharaj, Jizya, Zakat, Khums, Diwan departments
Harihara & Bukka, Krishnadeva Raya, Battle of Talikota, Bahmani
Battles of Panipat, Khanwa, Chanderi, Ghaghra; Tulughma tactic

table.topic: Mughal Empire – Humayun & Sher Shah Suri
table.sub_areas: Chausa, Kannauj, Dinpanah, Grand Trunk Road

table.topic: Mughal Empire – Jahangir, Shah Jahan, Aurangzeb
table.sub_areas: Taj Mahal, Noor Jahan, Jaziya, Bibi ka Maqbara
Nadir Shah, Ahmad Shah Abdali, Shivaji

subtopics.name: Key Facts
content: Birth: 971 AD in Zabulistan (Afghanistan). He was both Turk & Afghan.
Father: Sabuktigin (Turk); Mother: Local woman of Iran
Reign Period: 998–1030 AD; Capital: Ghazni (Afghanistan) #imp.2024
Attacked India 17 times between 1000–1027 AD

table.event: Punjab Attack – Pala king Jaipal defeated in Battle of Peshawar #imp
Multan Attack; defeated Anandapala (son of Jaipal) #imp
Plundered Mathura Temples; attacked Kannauj ruler Rajyapal #imp
Conquered Kannauj by defeating Chandela Gaur
Ghazni died due to Malaria and Tuberculosis

subtopics.name: Famous Writers at the Court of Ghaznavids
content: Al-Biruni – wrote Kitab-ul-Hind or Tehqiq-i-Hind (in Arabic)
Farrukhi Sisteni
Firdausi – wrote Shahnama (Full name: Abul-Qasim Firdowsi Tusi)
Muhammad Ibn Utbi – wrote Al-Kitab al-Yamini (in Arabic)

subtopics.name: Prithviraj Chauhan
content: Real Name: Prithviraj III / Rai Pithora
Dynasty: Chahamana; Ruled: Sapadalabha
Father/Mother: Someshwar Chauhan / Karpouri
Court Painter: Anirudh
Poet friend: Chandbardai (wrote Prithviraj Raso) #imp.2024
1191 – Battle of Tarain I – Prithviraj Won (Ghori attacked Tabarhinda/Bhatinda) #imp
1192 – Battle of Tarain II – Ghori Won #imp
1194 – Battle of Chadawar – Ghori Defeated Jai Chand #imp
Ananga Pala Tomar founded Delhi in 1052 AD & ruled Delhi before Prithviraj Chauhan
Bijoliya Inscription reveals Chauhans are Vats Gotriya Brahman #imp.2024

subtopics.name: Muhammad Ghori (Muhammad Bin Sang)
content: 1175 – Battle of Multan (Ghori defeated Moolraj II) #imp
1178 – Battle of Mt. Abu (Bhim Dev II won) #imp

subtopics.name: Rulers Reign Duration
content: Qutub-ud-din Aibak: 1206–1210 AD (4 yrs)
Iltutmish: 1211–1236 AD (25 yrs)
Razia Sultan: 1236–1240 AD (4 yrs)
Ghiyas-ud-din Balban: 1266–1287 (21 yrs)
Jalal-ud-din Firoz Khilji: 1290–1296 (6 yrs)
Alauddin Khilji: 1296–1316 (20 yrs)
Ghiyas-ud-din Tughlaq: 1320–1325 AD (5 yrs)
Muhammad Bin Tughlaq: 1325–1351 (26 yrs)
Firoz Shah Tughlaq: 1351–1388 AD (37 yrs)
Khizr Khan: 1414–1421 AD (7 yrs)
Muizuddin Mubarak Shah: 1421–1434 AD (13 yrs)
Bahlol Lodhi: 1451–1489 AD (38 yrs)
Sikander Lodhi (Nizam Khan): 1489–1517 AD (28 yrs)
Ibrahim Lodhi: 1517–1526 (9 yrs)

subtopics.name: Qutub-ud-din Aibak (1206–1210)
content: Founder of the Slave Dynasty
First to introduce Turkish culture in Delhi
Never accepted title of 'Sultan' #imp; Titles: Malik/SipahSalar/Lakh Baksh
Capital: Lahore
Buildings: Qutub Minar (73 m, Indo-Islamic, Red & buff Sandstone)
Quwwat-ul-Islam – India's 1st Mosque at Delhi (1192–1198 AD)
Adhai Din Ka Jhopra at Ajmer
Patronised Hasan-un-Nizami (author of Taj-ul-Massir) and Fakhr-i-Mudabbir
1210: Died during playing Chaugan/Polo #imp

subtopics.name: Shams-ud-Din Iltutmish (1211–1236 AD)
content: Real Founder of Delhi Sultanate #imp
First Sultan recognised by Caliph of Baghdad #imp
Shifted capital from Lahore to Delhi
Established Chahalgani/Chalisa (Group of 40 Nobles) #imp.2024
Completed construction of Qutub Minar #imp
Launched Iqta System (division of territories between Sultans and Nobility)
Khalisa: Areas directly under Sultan; Iqta: rest of areas
Introduced 2 coins: Tanka (silver) & Jital (copper) #imp.2024
Known as 'Father of Tomb Construction'; built Sultan Garhi & Hauz-i-Sultani #imp
Nominated Raziya as his successor; Initiated hereditary succession
Son: Rukn-ud-din Firuz; Daughter: Razia Sultan
Patronised Minhaj-ul-Siraj (author of Tabaqat-i-Nasiri)

subtopics.name: Razia Sultan (1236–1240 AD)
content: First and Last Woman Muslim ruler of Delhi #imp.2024
Did not accept 'Parda' #imp
Wore 'Quba' (Blazer) and 'Qula' (Cap) #imp
Commander: Jamal-ud-din Yaqut (Abyssian Slave)
Altunia killed Yaqut & imprisoned Razia in Qila Mubarak #imp
Husband: Malik Ikhtiaruddin Altunia (Governor of Bathinda/Tabarhinda)
Minhaj-ul-Siraj acknowledged Razia was more capable than her brothers

subtopics.name: Ghiyas-ud-din Balban (1266–1287)
content: Titles: Ulugh Khan, Naib-i-Mamlikat, Nasir-amir-ul-momin, Zil-i-ilahi, Zil-e-Khudai, Kingmaker
Killed/suppressed Chahalgani #imp
Introduced Nauroz Culture
Introduced Sijdah (Zaminbosi) and Paibos
Followed 'Blood and Iron' policy
Court Poets: Amir Khusro & Amir Hasan
Separated Diwan-i-Wizarat (Finance) from Diwan-i-Arz (Defence)
Appointed son Bughra Khan as Governor of Bengal after defeating Tughril Beg Khan
Prince Muhammad Khan died fighting Mongols

subtopics.name: Jalal-ud-din Firoz Khilji (1290–1296)
content: Founder of Khilji Dynasty

subtopics.name: Alauddin Khilji (1296–1316)
content: Real Name: Ali Gurshasp
Titles: Alexander Second/Sikander-i-Sani, Yamin-ul-Khilafat, Nasir-e-amir-ul-momini
Most Effective Sultan of Delhi Sultanate
Appointed as Amir-i-Tuzuk (Master of Ceremonies) & Ariz-i-Mumalik (Minister of Law) by Jalaluddin
Introduced Chehra system (ID Card) and 'Daag & Huliya' system
Introduced Land Measurement Unit: 'Biswa'
Introduced Market Control System; fixed Land Revenue in cash
3 Primary Taxes: Kharaj (50% land tax), Gharai (Housing Tax), Charai (tax on cattle); Jizya on non-Muslims
Malik Kafur ('Hazaar Dinari') – captured by Nusrat Khan in Gujarat conquest
Reorganised spy services (Barids); 'Kingship Knows No Kinship'
Court Poets: Amir Khusrau and Mir Hasan Dehlavi
Amir Khusrau: first poet to write about Jauhar in Persian
Created post of Diwan-i-Mustakhraj
Markets controlled by: Diwan-i-Riyasat (register merchants) & Shahana-i-Mandi (Mandi Controller)
Ziauddin Barani wrote Tarikh-i-FiruzShahi (about 6 Sultans)
Defeated Mongols at battles of Kili, Jalandhar, Ravi, and Amroha
Built Siri Fort (capital)

table.detail: 1st Southern Campaign; looted treasury to raise army against Jalal-ud-din
Ulugh Khan & Nusrat Khan defeated Vaghela King Karna; captured Queen Kamal Devi & Malik Kafur
Defeated King Hamir Deva; Nusrat Khan killed; Jauhar performed by Rajput ladies
Malik Kafur defeated Ramchandra Deva Yadava; given title Rai Raiyana
Malik Kafur defeated Kakatiya King Pratap Rudra Deva; received Koh-i-noor diamond

subtopics.name: Ghiyas-ud-din Tughlaq (1320–1325 AD)
content: Founder of Tughlaq Dynasty
Was Governor of Punjab (1320–1324) during reign of Ala-ud-din Khilji
Title: Ghazi Malik
Introduced Postal Services & Irrigation System
Built Tughlaqabad (city & strong fort) near Delhi
Amir Khusrau's Tughlaq Nama deals with his rise

subtopics.name: Muhammad Bin Tughlaq (1325–1351) – 'Paagal Raja'
content: 1325: New tax 'Kharaj-i-ala' on Doab region
1327: Changed Devgiri name to Daulatabad; shifted capital Delhi → Daulatabad
Severe famine broke out during his reign
1330: Token Currency – Copper coins
1333: Ibn-Batuta visited; wrote Rehla; appointed as Chief Qazi
Built fort of Adilabad, city of Jahanpanah, and Begumpuri Mosque
1338: Khorasan Project; 1339: Quarachill Expedition
Appointed common people to high posts: Aziz Khummar, Firuz Hajjam, Manka Tabbakh, Ladha & Pira
Established Diwan-i-Amir Kohi (Agriculture Department)
First Sultan to visit Ajmer shrine of Muinuddin Chishti
Harihara & Bukka established Vijayanagar Empire during his reign
Battle of Talikota (23 Jan 1565): Aliya Rama Raya vs Deccan Sultanates coalition; Vijayanagar collapsed, Hampi destroyed

subtopics.name: Firoz Shah Tughlaq (1351–1388 AD)
content: Built Feroz Shah Kotla Fort in 1354 (also known as home of djinns)
Created Diwan-i-Khairat (Dept. of Charity)
Ziauddin Barani (1285–1358): political thinker of Delhi Sultanate

subtopics.name: Sayyid Dynasty (1414–1450)
content: Khizr Khan (1414–1421 AD)
Muizuddin Mubarak Shah (1421–1434 AD)
Yahya bin Ahmad Sirhindi wrote Tarikh-i-Mubarak Shahi #imp.2024

subtopics.name: Lodhi Dynasty (1451–1526 AD)
content: Bahlol Lodhi (1451–1489 AD): 1st Afghani Sultan in Delhi Sultanate; Did Demonetisation; launched Bahloli Coin

Sikander Lodhi (Nizam Khan) (1489–1517 AD): Introduced Land Unit 'Gaz-e-Sikandari'; Established Agra in 1504 AD #imp.2024; Shifted capital Delhi → Agra in 1506; Abolished Zakat & Octroi Duty on grains; Built Moth ki Masjid (Double Dome Structure); Reimposed Jaziya; Pen name: 'Gulrukhi'
Ibrahim Lodhi (1517–1526): Defeated by Rana Sanga in Battle of Khatoli (1517); Lost First Battle of Panipat (1526) to Babur; Tomb in Panipat (Haryana)

chapters.chapter: Taxation System & Departments of Delhi Sultanate

table.detail: 1/5th of looted money given to Sultan #imp
Extra 2.5% from Muslims and 5% from Non-Muslims
Import & Export tax on Commercial Goods #imp

subtopics.name: Departments of Delhi Sultanate
content: Diwan-i-Wizarat: Finance Department (headed by Wazir)
Diwan-i-Risalat: Religious, Foreign Affairs & Appeals
Diwan-i-Arz: Defence Department
Diwan-i-Insha/Ashraf: Correspondence/Media/News
Diwan-i-Amir Kohi: Agriculture Department (by Muhammad Bin Tughlaq) #imp.2024
Diwan-i-Mustakhraz: Revenue/Arrears Department (by Alauddin Khilji)
Diwan-i-Khairat: Department of Charity (by Firoz Shah Tughlaq)
Diwan-i-Ishtiaq: Pension Department
Diwan-i-Bandagan: Department of Slaves (Bandagan = military slaves)
Diwan-i-Qaza-i-Mamalik: Department of Justice
Diwan-i-Waqoof: Expense/Revenue Department

subtopics.name: Vijayanagar Empire
content: Founded by Harihara & Bukka (defeated Madurai Sultanate under Muhammad Bin Tughlaq's reign)
Rulers order: Harihara I → Devaraya II → Krishnadeva Raya
Krishnadeva Raya: wrote Amuktamalyada (devotion to Lord Vishnu)
Rulers referred as 'Rayas' or 'Narapati' (lord of men)
Administration system: Amaram (Military fief)
Neither Iqta nor Mansabdari system was prevalent in Vijayanagar #imp
Battle of Raichur: Adil Shah of Bijapur defeated Krishnadeva Raya
Battle of Talikota (23 Jan 1565): Coalition of Deccan Sultanates (Bijapur, Ahmadnagar, Golconda, Bidar) vs Aliya Rama Raya; Vijayanagar destroyed, capital Hampi razed
Hazara Rama Temple: Private temple (not public)
Lotus Palace: Shows Persian (Indo-Islamic) architectural influence
Sacred center of the city: located south of Tungabhadra

subtopics.name: Deccan/Bahmani Sultanates
content: Gol Gumbaz: Tomb of Mohammed Adil Shah located in Karnataka
Golconda Fort: Located in Telangana (not Rajasthan)
Chand Bibi: Regent of Ahmadnagar; heroically resisted Mughals; associated with Bijapur through marriage

subtopics.name: Key Facts
content: Real Name: Zahir-ud-din Muhammad Babur
Founder of Mughal Empire in India
Related to Timur (father's side) and Chengiz Khan (mother's side) #imp
Succeeded his father as ruler of Farghana (Uzbekistan) in 1494
Seized Kabul in 1504
Wives: Maham Begum, Aisha Sultan
Sister: Khanzada Begum
1st attack: Bhera
Autobiography: Baburnama (Turkish)
Popularised Gunpowder; Cavalry & artillery tactics: Tulughma and Araba
Built Aram Bagh (oldest Mughal Garden) in 1528 in Agra
Introduced Charbagh and symmetrically laid out gardens
Mughal gardens inspired by Persian idea of paradise gardens

table.result: Defeated Army of Bengal and Bihar

subtopics.name: Death
content: Died: 26 Dec 1530
Tomb: First at Arambagh in Agra, later in Kabul
First Mughal Emperor buried outside India

subtopics.name: Key Facts
content: Real Name: Mirza Nasir-ud-Din Muhammad (eldest son of Babur) #imp
Wives: Bega Begum, Hamida Banu Begum #imp
Brothers: Kamran Mirza, Hindal Mirza, Askari Mirza, Khalil Mirza
Humayun controlled Delhi, Agra & Central India; Kamran controlled Afghanistan & Punjab #imp.2024
Title: Insan-i-Kamil (Perfect man)
1531 – Devra: Humayun defeated Mahmud Lodi #imp
1539 – Chausa: Sher Shah Suri won #imp.2024
1540 – Kannauj/Belgram: Sher Shah Suri won #imp
1555 – Sirhind: Defeated Sikander Shah Suri #imp
Sister Gul Badan Begum wrote Humayun-Nama
Brought painters Mir Sayyid Ali and Abdal Samad to India
Built Dinpanah (new city at Delhi, on bank of River Yamuna)
Built Sher-e-Mandal (Library)
Widow Biga Begum built Humayun's Tomb (UNESCO site)

chapters.chapter: Mughal Empire – Akbar, Jahangir, Shah Jahan & Aurangzeb

subtopics.name: Aurangzeb (1658–1707)
content: Real Name: Muhi al-Din Muhammad
Titles: Zinda Peer (Living Saint), Shahi Darvesh
Wife: Dilras Banu, Rabia-ul-Daurani #imp
1658: Captured Shahjahan; coronation ceremony
1658–59: Battle of Dharmat/Deorai – defeated Dara Shikoh #imp
1659: Coronation in Red Fort; Abolished Nauroz Festival #imp
1667: Gave French permission to build factory at Surat
1668: Muhammad Kazim compiled Alamgirnama (first 10 years of Aurangzeb's reign)
1669: Abolished Jharokha Darshan (Public Audience) & terminated all Musicians #imp
1670: Abolished Tuladan System #imp
1675: Executed 9th Sikh Guru Teg Bahadur #imp.2024
1679: Re-imposed Jaziya Tax & Pilgrim Tax #imp
Annexed Bijapur (1686) under Sikander Adil Shah
Annexed Golconda (1687) against Qutub Shahi dynasty
Attempted to vandalize Kailasa Temple at Ellora in 1682 #imp
1707: Tarikh-i-Dilkusha written by Bhimsen in Persian (translated by Jadunath Sarkar) #imp
Masir-i-Alamgiri by Mustaid Khan throws light on Aurangzeb's rule #imp
Not tolerant of other Muslim sects; stopped celebration of Muharram
Appointed Rajputs to high positions; Mughal Empire achieved largest territorial limits under him #imp.2024
Fatwa-i-Alamgiri (major Islamic law compilation) commissioned by Aurangzeb
Buildings: Moti Masjid (at Red Fort); Bibi ka Maqbara/Black Taj Mahal/Taj of Deccan built by Azamshah (son) in Aurangabad (1668–69) #imp

table.facts: Wife of Jahangir; exercised real political power; farmans in her name; coins minted; led 'Noor Jahan Junta'; patron of art & architecture
Brave Gond queen; ruled Jabalpur region; fought Mughal general Asaf Khan; chose martyrdom over surrender
Regent of Ahmadnagar; heroically resisted Mughals; associated with Bijapur through marriage; led fort defense
First & Last woman Muslim ruler of Delhi; wore Quba & Qula; did not observe Parda; imprisoned in Qila Mubarak

subtopics.name: Muhammad Shah Rangeela (1719–1748)
content: Autonomous states emerged: Nizam-ul-Mulk (Deccan), Saadat Khan (Awadh), Murshid Quli Khan (Bengal, Bihar, Orissa)
Nadir Shah invaded India and looted Delhi; took away Kohinoor Diamond
Nadir Shah defeated Muhammad Shah in Battle of Karnal in 1739 #imp
Ahmad Shah Abdali raided Delhi for first time during his reign
Became king with help of Sayyid Brothers; later killed them with help of Nizam-ul-Mulk (Asaf Jah)

subtopics.name: Shivaji & Maratha Empire
content: Shivaji's exploits against Mughals and Bijapur made him a legendary figure
Aurangzeb led Deccan campaign against Marathas and spent long time in the region

table.builder: Started by Qutub-ud-din Aibak, completed by Iltutmish
table.style: Indo-Islamic; Red & buff sandstone; Height 73m; Base diameter 14.32m
Taj of Deccan / Black Taj Mahal

subtopics.name: Architectural Features
content: True arch and dome first introduced in India by the Delhi Sultanate
Bengal and Jaunpur developed distinctive architectural styles distinct from Delhi Sultanate
Regional styles often blended Islamic structures with local artistic traditions
Mughal gardens inspired by Persian idea of paradise gardens (Charbagh)
